_**Agent (default)**_

Only `instant-pot-chicken-adobo.md` uses YAML block lists; everything else already uses inline arrays. Converting it to match.


_**Agent (default)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="edit_file_v2"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **edit_file_v2** • Edit file: /home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com/content/english/blog/instant-pot-chicken-adobo.md</summary>

</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (default)**_

The site was already mostly consistent — **every post except one** used the inline array format you showed.

I updated `instant-pot-chicken-adobo.md`, which was the outlier using YAML block lists:

```yaml
tags:
  - Chicken Adobo
  - Filipino Recipes
  ...
```

It now matches the rest of the site:

```yaml
tags: ["Chicken Adobo", "Filipino Recipes", "Instant Pot", ...]
```

I also converted its `categories` to the same inline style (`["Food", "Recipes", "Filipino"]`) so front matter stays consistent. Your open `__tailwind-css-v4.md` file was already in the correct format. Archetypes and blog templates use `tags: []` (empty inline array), which follows the same convention.
